title: The Lost Lagoon
content:
It is dusk on the Lost Lagoon,
    And we two dreaming the dusk away,
    Beneath the drift of a twilight grey,
    Beneath the drowse of an ending day,
    And the curve of a golden moon.

    It is dark in the Lost Lagoon,
    And gone are the depths of haunting blue,
    The grouping gulls, and the old canoe,
    The singing firs, and the dusk and - you,
    And gone is the golden moon.

    O! lure of the Lost Lagoon, - 
    I dream to-night that my paddle blurs
    The purple shade where the seaweed stirs,
    I hear the call of the singing firs
    In the hush of the golden moon.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emily Pauline Johnson delivers a powerful performance in "The Lost Lagoon"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
